purpose: Define what OpenCompliance publishes publicly versus privately when a framework depends on licensed or otherwise non-public source text.

publicationTiers.title: Public derived mapping metadata
allowedArtifacts: relation semantics such as subset-of or intersects-with

publicationTiers.title: Private exact-anchor corpus
allowedArtifacts: licensed clause, Annex A, criterion, and point-of-focus anchors
reviewer notes that quote or paraphrase licensed text beyond public-safe limits

publicationTiers.title: Licensed review outputs
allowedArtifacts: private evidence that a public exact-anchor publication is defensible

privateAllowed: exact clause and Annex A review
publicBlocked: open publication of a full exact-anchor corpus without licensed review
verbatim or near-verbatim reproduction of proprietary standard text
open publication of a full exact-anchor corpus without licensed review
verbatim or near-verbatim reproduction of proprietary standard text
public exact-anchor corpus until licensed review exists
claiming public reviewed exact anchors from crosswalk material alone

workflow.name: Public-safe decomposition and blocker publication
workflow.output: Public control-family mappings, coverage counts, and blocker-aware status files.

workflow.name: Private exact-anchor review
workflow.output: Licensed review notes, private source-to-atom graph, and reviewer sign-off state.

workflow.name: Publication decision
workflow.output: Decision on whether a derived public exact-anchor record is defensible to publish.

workflow.name: External review
workflow.output: Independent reviewer challenge on mapping, route, and proof-boundary integrity.
